requirements.description: Content creation & SEO validation: Support Article and Page types with mandatory Title, Body, Slug, and SEO meta description.
Content Lifecycle & Workflow: Enforce Draft -> Review -> Publish state transitions and prevent invalid state changes.
Audit Logging: Every create, update, delete, and state change action shall be logged in an append-only audit trail.
Public API: Expose read-only endpoints for published content items with caching headers.
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Enforce exactly 3 roles (Admin, Editor, Author) and prevent deletion of the last Admin account.
Security & Token Lifecycle: Issue JWT access and refresh tokens, invalidate refresh tokens upon logout or deactivation, enforce account lockout.
Authenticate users via email and password.
Enforce passwords of minimum length 12 characters, containing at least one letter and one digit.
Hash passwords using Argon2id/bcrypt. Plaintext passwords never logged or stored.
Issue JWT access token and refresh token upon successful login.
Invalidate refresh tokens upon password change, user deactivation, or explicit logout.
Lock account after 5 failed login attempts within 10 minutes.
Provide password reset flow via one-time email link valid for 60 minutes.
Password reset links shall be single-use and invalidated after use or expiry.
Allow users to log out and invalidate refresh token server-side.
Failed login attempts and successful logins written to audit log.
Only Admin role can create, edit, deactivate, or delete user accounts.
Invite users by email with one-time link to set initial password.
Each user has exactly one role: Admin, Editor, or Author.
Prevent deletion of the last remaining Admin account.
Deactivated users denied login immediately while authored content remains intact.
Display list of users with name, email, role, status, and last login.
Email addresses unique system-wide and RFC 5322 compliant.
All user mutation actions written to audit log.
Enforce permission matrix on every protected endpoint at route and data access layers.
Authors can only create, view, edit, and submit their own content.
Editors can view, edit, publish, and unpublish all content regardless of author.
Admins have all Editor permissions plus user and audit log management.
Unauthorized requests return HTTP 403, unauthenticated return HTTP 401.
Public API only exposes content with status Published and published_at <= current time.
Support content types Article and Page.
Every content item has unique URL-safe slug auto-generated from title.
Prevent slug collisions within a content type.
Content transitions only along defined lifecycle states.
Content saveable as Draft with only Title populated.
Publishing requires non-empty Title, Body, Slug, and SEO meta description.
Support scheduled publish by setting scheduled_at timestamp.
Retain version history on every save with snapshot and editor identity.
Allow reverting content to prior version by creating a new version.
Support listing content with filters and pagination.
Support case-insensitive title search on content list.
Unpublishing removes item from public API within 60 seconds.
Deletion is soft delete retained for 30 days.
Rich text Body stored in structured JSON/sanitized format.
Rich text editor supports standard formatting elements.
All content mutation actions written to audit log.
Support uploading PNG, JPEG, WEBP, GIF, SVG, PDF.
Maximum file size 20 MB per file (configurable).
Reject uploads whose MIME type, extension, or magic bytes do not match allow-list.
Uploads use presigned URLs directly to object storage.
Generate responsive variants (thumbnail, medium) per image.
Media items store editable alt text, caption, and metadata.
Prevent deletion of media item referenced by active content.
Media library supports search by filename and filters by uploader/date.
Content items include SEO Title, Meta Description, Slug, and Social Share Image.
Default SEO Title to content Title and Meta Description to first 160 characters.
Public API includes all SEO fields in single-item responses.
Record audit events with timestamp, actor, IP, action, target, and change summary.
Audit actions include auth, user management, content changes, and media changes.
Audit log is append-only without API/UI to modify or delete.
Audit log retained for at least 365 days.
Admins can view audit log filtered by actor, action, target, and date range.
Audit log UI paginated and exportable as CSV.
Public API exposes read-only endpoints.
Authentication via API key passed in X-API-Key header.
Public API returns only Published content with published_at <= current time.
Responses include cache headers.
Support pagination via page and page_size query parameters.
Standardized error shape with code, message, and details.
OpenAPI 3.1 spec kept in sync with implementation.
